title: epipactis atrorubens subsp. subclausa
intro: Epipactis atrorubens subsp. subclausa, a sub-species of the Helleborine orchid, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which have been attributed to its high concentration of glycosides and flavonoids. The health benefits of Epipactis atrorubens subsp. subclausa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, tinctures, and decoctions to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
